nodes.x: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding put forth her voice?
Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, and liftest up thy voice for understanding;

For behold, the Lord doth grant unto all nations, of their own nation and tongue, to teach his word, yea, in wisdom, all that he seeth fit that they should have; therefore we see that the Lord doth counsel in wisdom, according to that which is just and true.
Her strengthen idiot’s boast ;her wisdom, blind ; Her gain, eternal loss; her hope, a dream;
Because God hath deprived her of wisdom, neither hath he imparted to her understanding.
Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;
She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths.
For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,
She dwelleth and abideth on the rock, upon the crag of the rock, and the strong place.
She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.
For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.
She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying,
She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city,
She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths.
¶ Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
Unto you, O men, I call; and my voice is to the sons of man.
And verily, verily, I say unto you, that this is my voice unto all. Amen.
Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.
Behold, I, the Lord, utter my voice, and it shall be obeyed.
I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O Lord.
Moreover he said unto me, Son of man, all my words that I shall speak unto thee receive in thine heart, and hear with thine ears.
O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.
Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known.
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
Ye have not applied your hearts to understanding; therefore, ye have not been wise. Therefore, what teach ye this people?
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness.
Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.
My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.
Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.
My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
Give ear, O ye heavens, and I will speak; and hear, O earth, the words of my mouth.
My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord: but the words of the pure are pleasant words.
Yea, wo be unto you because of your wickedness and abominations!
The wicked is snared by the transgression of his lips: but the just shall come out of trouble.
The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.
All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing froward or perverse in them.
¶ Hearken unto me, ye stouthearted, that are far from righteousness:
I have sworn by myself, the word is gone out of my mouth in righteousness, and shall not return, That unto me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear.
My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long: for they are confounded, for they are brought unto shame, that seek my hurt.
My tongue shall speak of thy word: for all thy commandments are righteousness.
They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.
Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
He that chastiseth the heathen, shall not he correct? he that teacheth man knowledge, shall not he know?
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
He that keepeth his commandments receiveth truth and light, until he is glorified in truth and knoweth all things.
Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.
How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver!
My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.
It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.
For the merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.
If I have made gold my hope, or have said to the fine gold, Thou art my confidence;
For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.
She is more precious than rubies: and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.
No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom is above rubies.
There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be entreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.
I wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions.
And shall find wisdom and great treasures of knowledge, even hidden treasures;
And hourly treasure up within my heart Wisdom and knowledge that will not decay ;
The lips of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish doeth not so.
The fear of the Lord is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.
The fear of the Lord is clean, enduring for ever: the judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.

“Fear,” said he, “the Lord, and keep His 
commandments.’ For if you keep the com- 
mandments of God, you will be powerful in 
every action, and every one of your actions will 
be incomparable. For, fearing the Lord, you 
will do all things well. This is the fear which 
you ought to have, that you may be saved. But 
fear not the devil; for, fearing the Lord, you 
will have dominion over the devil,…
The fear of the Lord tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.
Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength.
With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.
Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.
For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
Thy testimonies also are my delight and my counsellors.
Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,
By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.
Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth.
Kings of the earth, and all people; princes, and all judges of the earth:
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth.
By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.
Remember the kings, the princes, the nobles, and the great ones of the earth, and all people, and the churches, all the poor, the needy, and afflicted ones of the earth;
I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me.
He that seeketh me early shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.
Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
They also that seek after my life lay snares for me: and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and imagine deceits all the day long.
And when you have found them you shall show these things unto them.
For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them.
Riches and honour are with me; yea, durable riches and righteousness.
By humility and the fear of the Lord are riches, and honour, and life.
Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
Both riches and honour come of thee, and thou reignest over all; and in thine hand is power and might; and in thine hand it is to make great, and to give strength unto all.
A gracious woman retaineth honour: and strong men retain riches.
Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death.
My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.
Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.
More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold: sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.
I lead in the way of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of judgment:
Lead me, O Lord, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.
Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
Open to me the gates of righteousness: I will go into them, and I will praise the Lord:
Thus saith the Lord, Keep ye judgment, and do justice: for my salvation is near to come, and my righteousness to be revealed.
That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
That I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasures.

that I may have the Spirit of God, with the prayers and confidence of the people, to speak in wisdom that which is necessary, and then give opportunity to my brethren; for I love to hear them, and so do this people.
And I will forsake the remnant of mine inheritance, and deliver them into the hand of their enemies; and they shall become a prey and a spoil to all their enemies;
To turn aside the needy from judgment, and to take away the right from the poor of my people, that widows may be their prey, and that they may rob the fatherless!
That the residue of men might seek after the Lord, and all the Gentiles, upon whom my name is called, saith the Lord, who doeth all these things.
The Lord possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.
Then the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
And he said, Wherefore doth my lord thus pursue after his servant? for what have I done? or what evil is in mine hand?
Then the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,

Wherefore, because that Satan rebelled against me, and sought to destroy the agency of man, which I, the Lord God, had given him, and also, that I should give unto him mine own power; by the power of mine Only Begotten, I caused that he should be cast down;
Then came the word of the Lord unto me, saying,
I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was.
Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever thou hadst formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, thou art God.
In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.

Although my house be not so with God; yet he hath made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all things, and sure: for this is all my salvation, and all my desire, although he make it not to grow.
And immediately I was in the spirit: and, behold, a throne was set in heaven, and one sat on the throne.
When there were no depths, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains abounding with water.
And the earth was without form, and void; and I caused darkness to come up upon the face of the deep; and my Spirit moved upon the face of the water; for I am God.
And it came to pass that many were drowned in the depths of the fountain; and many were lost from his view, wandering in strange roads.
And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.
The fountains also of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped, and the rain from heaven was restrained;
Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth:
I beheld the mountains, and, lo, they trembled, and all the hills moved lightly.
And upon all the high mountains, and upon all the hills that are lifted up,

A mountain or round hill, perhaps 500 feet in height, rises in
the midst of the town, and is surrounded on all sides by the level
of the fertile, well watered and well cultivated plain. This hill
is near our residence, easy of access, and commands a view of the
whole valley with its farms, orchards, vineyards, towns, streets,
river and water ditches, fertile as Eden and stretching away till
lost…
While as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the highest part of the dust of the world.
And he said: Yea, I believe that he created all things which are in the earth; but I do not know the heavens.

And he did expound all things, even from the beginning until the time that he should come in his glory—yea, even all things which should come upon the face of the earth, even until the elements should melt with fervent heat, and the earth should be wrapt together as a scroll, and the heavens and the earth should pass away;
All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.
And he that sat on the cloud thrust in his sickle on the earth; and the earth was reaped.
When he prepared the heavens, I was there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth:
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
And it came to pass that I turned and went up on the mount; and as I stood upon the mount, I beheld the heavens open, and I was clothed upon with glory;
And it came to pass that he saw One descending out of the midst of heaven, and he beheld that his luster was above that of the sun at noon-day.
And saw heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth:
When he established the clouds above: when he strengthened the fountains of the deep:

same day were all the fountains of the great deep broken up, and the windows of heaven were opened.” What was done? “The windows of heaven were opened,” and the immense bodies of waters that exist in the upper firmament were let down, or as it is expressed, “the windows of heaven were opened.” What else? “The same day were all the fountains of the great deep broken up.” You have got a Brigham…
At the brightness that was before him his thick clouds passed, hail stones and coals of fire.
He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him were dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
When he gave to the sea his decree, that the waters should not pass his commandment: when he appointed the foundations of the earth:
He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.

18. And through that oath the sea was created,
    And †as its foundation† He set for it the sand against the time of
       (its) anger,
    And it dare not pass beyond it from the creation of the world unto
       eternity.
And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,
Nevertheless, unto whom is given power to command the waters, unto him it is given by the Spirit to know all his ways;
Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;
Rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth; and my delights were with the sons of men.
And he spake unto me, saying: Yea, and the most joyous to the soul.
He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.
And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was comforted in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rejoiced the more.
And he brought forth his people with joy, and his chosen with gladness:
Rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth; and my delights were with the sons of men.
Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;
Who so loved the world that he gave his own life, that as many as would believe might become the sons of God. Wherefore you are my son;
Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways.
¶ Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
O hearken, ye elders of my church, and give ear to the words which I shall speak unto you.
Hearken, O ye people of my church; for verily I say unto you that these things were spoken unto you for your profit and learning.
But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.

For behold, thus saith the Lord God: I will give unto the children of men line upon line, precept upon precept, here a little and there a little; and blessed are those who hearken unto my precepts, and lend an ear unto my counsel, for they shall learn wisdom; for unto him that receiveth I will give more; and from them that shall say, We have enough, from them shall be taken away even that which…
Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.
Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.
Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away.

Be wise in the days of your probation; strip yourselves of all uncleanness; ask not, that ye may consume it on your lusts, but ask with a firmness unshaken, that ye will yield to no temptation, but that ye will serve the true and living God.
Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.
Blessed is the man that trusteth in the Lord, and whose hope the Lord is.
Blessed be the Lord, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.
Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me.

Having been called upon this afternoon, to speak to this congregation, I earnestly desire that I may be so influenced by the spirit of truth that I may be able to bring forth such things as will be profitable for us to reflect upon. I feel that we are greatly blessed in being privileged to meet in this house, dedicated to the worship and service of our Heavenly Father, where we can
For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the Lord.
Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the Lord.
He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.
And whoso layeth down his life in my cause, for my name’s sake, shall find it again, even life eternal.
So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.

profit. Let us desire and pray for these things which will do us good, trusting in the Lord, seeking to know and do his will, and we shall come off conquerors and be crowned with crowns of glory, immortality, and eternal lives in the celestial kingdom
But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: all they that hate me love death.
He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.
The Lord trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.
He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.
The merciful man doeth good to his own soul: but he that is cruel troubleth his own flesh.
